title: Rome
content:
Above the circus of the world she sat,
    Beautiful and base, a harlot crowned with pride:
    Fierce nations, upon whom she sneered and spat,
    Shrieked at her feet and for her pastime died.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Madison Julius Cawein delivers a powerful performance in "Rome"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
